reviews.review_description: Woke up stepped outside got jumped by aliens with laser swords ran inside same thing happened in arken age 10/10 would recommend alright tho all jokes aside the graphics are great 👍 and upgrading you weapon is super satisfying and it’s not op like they are all pretty Balenced with  like less battery more dmg stuff like that and the game gives a decent challenge so it’s not like you reach the final boss in 1hr I’ve played 2-3hrs and I haven’t gotten to the first boss you just can’t give up in this game and what I would like to see in the future is like maybe a mode for when you start a new run you can do like challenge mode and it has like a death penalty that you can choose like Perma death, which would be really hard or something like lose all your money when you die stuff like that you know but overall this game is a 10 out of 10 ￼

This is the game I keep coming back to play over and over  I also watch others (a wolf in vr) play the game and it’s so fun to watch as well. The physics are outstanding. The climbing was so fun, I think the picks were my favorite, how easy they were to summon was a huge hit for me. I need a sequel. PLEASE!

developer_response.body: Thanks for leaving a review! A Wolf in VR's content is awesome, loved watching his play-through of Arken Age. Really glad you enjoyed the game! Thanks, Blake -VitruviusVR

reviews.review_description: Early review as new to this game. Rave reviews, poor graphics! That's my initial feeling..since trying for the second time to like this game, 3 hours into playing it I feel this game is growing on me. I'm am admittedly a graphics snob but also appreciate gameplay above all else but it takes patience with Arken age as I couldn't get my head around the graphics, they are vert good for a stand alone console. I will update the review but encourage anybody to give this game a good try before writing it off! It may well pull you in like it has me🙂 .

reviews.review_title: about aa good as vr can get
reviews.review_description: This is simply one of the best VR games I've ever played. When you consider what a small development team made this game it's absolutely shocking. It's right up there with AAA Developers. This game has great Gunplay great physics a great story if I'm going to criticize anything it's probably the soundtrack other than that I loved everything about the game. Whenever you see this kind of skill on display you start questioning. What would these developers interpretation of a Dungeon Crawler look like? What would their interpretation of a tactical shooter look like? What would their interpretation of a parkour type of game look like? Your mind just starts to drifting. I can't wait to see what they develop in the future I've Heard arcan age Two is a thing I can't wait for that.

reviews.review_title: I was wrong about this game, it's awesom
reviews.review_description: When I first tried this game I didn't like it. The physics engine threw me off a little. But then when I actually gave it a try I notice how beautifully polished every single mechanism in the game is and how well crafted the world is and then i noticed how amazing the physics engine is to. What surprised me the most is how much secrets and unlockables there are. Also to be able to freely go back to old zones gives it a sense of free roam. For a while the game felt like a free roam game. This game has got to be one of the best single player campaign games on the quest. If ur looking for a decent single player game with a really good campaign this is it. Story driven campaign, guns, swords, and puzzles. Like a futurist version of Zelda

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR games ever
reviews.review_description: During a time when VR game studios are closing and doing away with the big budget games like AW2, this incredible game is proof that VR can still have a future. Absolutely beautiful game, the gameplay mechanics are spectacular, both the shooting and the melee combat. Story is a little light, but you buy this game for the combat and action experience, not the story. Just a remarkable gem of a game made in a time of VR uncertainty which deserves everyone's support to ensure games like this can continue to be made and carry the platform.

I don't normally leave reviews but THIS I have to. This game was easily one of the best games I've ever played. The map, combat, and the story were better than most games I play. I was so invested in the story. I'm a lover of vr games with a good length campaign and a good story, this game had both. I HIGHLY RECOMMEND THIS GAME!

developer_response.body: It's so awesome to hear this, thanks for leaving a review! Really glad you enjoyed the game! Thanks, Blake -VitruviusVR

reviews.review_description:
In my opinion, this is how you design a game for VR. The controls are one of the best I’ve ever seen in a VR game and for other developers: If you have a game with a weapon with a scope - THIS is how you implement it. 
The game looks absolutely fantastic on Quest standalone. The story is maybe the weakest point but does not change the experience at all.
It is an awesome game and I hope the developers will make a DLC or a part 2.

This game has one of the best problems: There's not enough of it.

 Tiny critique though, I do wish the heavy gun was stronger. It felt like it required a lot of chest shots to bring an enemy down. 

Dlc please.
Game two please.

The game is incredibly well made and a lot of fun to play. The world has been crafted with so much beauty while the combat is gory and beastial. The game also has one of the most enjoyable climbing mechanics. The plot is interesting and driven well but you don't need to follow it to enjoy the game. Boss battles are also satisfyingly challenging

I really enjoyed this game, even though it had a lot of fighting, which I’m not good at. But at least I could come back from the dead and keep moving forward in the game. Beautiful game! Beautiful graphics as everyone has said. I hope there’s a sequel. oh, and I just want to add, there are no issues! Thank you testers

It's amazing, really. But then the ending happens and it's this.. very open ended nothing, a set up for a sequel that pretty much has your in-game boss going "well, you did it, kinda! I'm leaving now to set up for the next game, k-bai. Have fun with yourself."
This is the reason you bought a Quest 3/3S if you like singleplayer games. The art/visuals in this game and the gameplay are amazing. I hope you buy this!
Easily top 3 greatest games on Quest. Feels amazing! I love the pace and action of this jewel. Worth every penny. ❤️
Best games ever so far I played 15 of probably the best games on the quest three and by far this one is the best game ever. I wish there was more games like this. ￼
Graphics peak, mechanics peak, guns peak. One suggestion for the devs, what if there was a stealth kill you could do with the climbing picks!

developer_response.body: Hey thanks for the great review! And that's an awesome idea for the climbing picks, we'll think about how we can integrate that into the game! Cheers, Blake -VitruviusVR

reviews.review_description:
I have been playing quest combat/story games for years now and I think this game takes the cake. The  story, combat and exploration are so evenly balanced and paired with a beautiful fantasy world. This is a very complex game that was borderline overwhelming at first with how much you can do, but once I figured it out it just felt like the possibilities were endless. 
Firstly, the combat is amazing. The enemies have many different kinds and can attack in many different ways. You have an arsenal of weaponry that is readily available with one reach or button. Being able to summon enemies in certain areas is a great touch too.
The combat is great, but what really made this game so fun was the world. There are at least 20-25 different “maps” or “areas” that are all unique. You can climb, swim and crawl to explore countless secret passages. Every time I was ready to enter a new area I was so excited to see what it will look like. 
This game is great for many reasons and if you are looking for a fantasy combat/story game than this is for you.

This is truly one of the best VR games out there. It’s a wonderfully crazy mix of Metroid, Quake, Stride, melee combat, and exploration — all blended together with great care, love, and impressive attention to detail and mechanics. It’s a very polished experience, and it’s hard to imagine how much time went into refining it.

And yes, I highly recommend playing on Hard difficulty — that’s where the game really shines. You’ll start adapting your tactics to different enemies, choosing the right weapon mods for bosses, and developing your own strategies.
Every interaction feels amazing from swimming to climbing. Fighting feels incredible graphics amazing I can’t find a gripe for this game 10 out of 10

This is a must-play VR game for several reasons. The story is well crafted and keeps you interested throughout the experience. The mechanics make excellent use of VR, creating a level of immersion that feels natural and engaging. On top of that, the visuals are stunning and truly make you feel like you’re exploring an alien world. A unique and highly recommended experience.

This game is dang near a masterpiece, no, it IS a masterpiece. But it’s so so soooo underrated, not enough people are talking about this game. At a game this quality, you’d think it’d have like a larger group of fans but nope! Other people are too busy playing gtag clones and brainrot games to give these genuine A-grade quality vr games a chance. Also one suggestion, might be a big ask, could yall add an endless arena mode, or a roguelike mode? That kind of update would be perfect for this game🙏 12/10 game, it’s up there with the vr greats.
If you have doubts dont worry it is amazing

I finished the entire game and really enjoyed the experience. The world design is beautiful and immersive, and the environments make you feel like you’re inside a living alien ecosystem. Combat feels smooth and satisfying, and the VR mechanics are very well done.

The campaign kept me interested all the way to the end, with good pacing and creative bosses. I also appreciated the exploration elements and the way the game encourages you to interact with the environment.

Overall, it’s a very polished VR shooter and one of the more memorable VR games I’ve played. Definitely worth playing if you enjoy immersive adventure shooters.

Arkan Age is the first VR game in years that made me rethink what’s actually possible on the Meta platform. The graphics are stunning, the world feels alive, and the fluid mechanics put most other VR titles to shame. It proves that smooth interaction, polished physics, and high‑fidelity visuals aren’t “too hard for VR” — they just require real craftsmanship. After playing this, all the usual excuses for jank fall flat. Arkan Age shows it can be done, and it raises the bar for everyone else.
This is the first game that I 100% and that's causeI loved it and wanted to play it so much I really hope you guys make a sequel

Nothing to add other than yep it’s brilliant .

Immersion is at its heart throughout and there is much to challenge you as you explore this richly detailed biological ,fauna heavy , world.

Huge amount to get to grips with from the start with just the right balance of tutorial and work-it-out for yourself .

Becoming fluid with weapon load-outs , ammo swaps , mele and long range tactics and the best way to utilise junk , fruit and other collectibles does take time. Perseverance is key.

Using smelters to gain cash , fruit popping for health ( really satisfying hand- on method ) and the weapon upgrade stations are clever and feel “ real” rather than just add-ons.

The hand axes are superb and once you get the correct action to flick them open you really do have a very nifty way to climb and explore.

Character interactions are fairly standard VR fare but the detail built into each character is simply superb . Combat is what gaming combat always has been , ok but eventually relentless .
In a 2.5 x 2.5 m room space you do start to feel restricted in your sword swinging arcs , foes getting too crowded to allow you ample space to evade without crashing into your real furniture.

I am playing on the easiest setting , enough challenge for me and with the wobbly energy beam I can dispatch at a distance without them getting too close.

So far it’s great , have depleted at least 5 full battery charges without feeling time has dragged. 

It’s green , lush , immersive and fab.

Enjoy.

I didn’t  like it at first because it all looks the same to me and it just felt bland. However, I gave it more time and realized that there really aren’t many standalone games that are an actual GAME. This is for sure a full fledged game. It doesn’t just depend on decent graphics and gore, it’s an actual GAME, which sadly is rare in VR, especially standalone.

I'm not a gamer and got the Quest for fitness, but this one caught my eye and I'm hooked. It is just the right difficulty for me, though it can be played on higher difficulties, which I will probably do when I get through. So much fun.

Just completed my first full playthrough on normal difficulty. I can see why there are so many positive reviews.

The devs have created an interesting, rich, organic world that is fun to explore. I managed to get a 100% completion and find every collectable and weapon upgrade, but I am very much the completionist type.

The other main pros:
* The weapon and upgrade system is fun and engaging. You get just a melee, light, and heavy weapon if you exclude your climbing pickaxes. But each weapon is modular with you removing and adding bits while you gain extra bonuses if you complete a set of a particular weapon type. I found myself experimenting a lot, and some challenges encourage you to use certain types for extra loot.
* Solid graphics and performance. I understand that the devs spent about 10 months porting this to the Quest 3 after initial release on PCVR and PSVR2, and honestly, it shows. Sure, there are some visual downgrades like a lack of shadows and translucent water for a mobile chip, but the game still looks good, and the resolution is decent. I experienced zero stutter or frame drops throughout my entire playthrough and zero crashes. I did find one oddity on the map known as "Tomb of Corix," where I definitely managed to climb to somewhere I shouldn't have done and then jumped off the map completely to my doom. But the game just restarted to the last save point.
* Combat is engaging. I found the melee a bit "clunky" at times but fun while having more success with the ranged weapons. Maybe, just my play style. The enemies, for the most part, seem to have decent AI. Oddly, it seems they're rather shortsighted at times when they're just roaming around (apart from the battle arena type stages where they actively hunt you from afar), but once you have their attention, they won't mindlessly run towards you. They'll often use the scenery and take cover and attack you from various angles.
* NPCs and enemies look really good with lots of detail and solid animations. You can blow chunks of armour off the enemies, dismember, and decapitate them. I often found myself pausing to see if they would climb off the floor, not sure whether they were stunned or dead.
* Berry popping is oddly satisfying to make your health syringes. You'll know what I mean in due course.
* You can customise all your weaponry with your own spray paint job at work bench. It's a great touch. The one thing that maybe the devs could have made clearer is which paint coat of the five you use affects which part of the weapon cosmetic. It wasn't obvious to me until I used it. A very small niggle though.
* Plenty of collectables, which encourages exploration.
* Epic boss battles and particularly at the end; surprisingly, I defeated the last boss with a single attempt, but that's likely because I was well into the swing of things by then and chose the right weapon. Before that, I kicked the bucket a few times.

Overall, thank you very much to the devs. This is how a VR game should be done. I picked this up on sale but felt by the end that it was well worth the full asking price. I hope it achieves the success and sales figures that it deserves so we can have a sequel that was set up nicely at the end.
This game has scratched that itch of old PS1 and N64 platformers/collectors. This has been a blast and is the first adventure game in VR that has trult kept my attention.

reviews.review_title: Best Game for three fingered people
reviews.review_description: This is the one to have. I don’t know why this game is so perfect, but the gameplay and controls are top notch. It’s a must own. Nothing feels janky or awkward. This is the most “natural” experience I’ve had except maybe Asgards Wrath 2. But honestly I think even this game feels more tangible in the virtual world.

reviews.review_title: Best game outside of Ashard's Wrath 2!
reviews.review_description:
This blew my mind. I've been waiting for someone to take the Asgard's Wrath 2 play and create something new. 

Graphically this is phenomenal. 

The audio is also phenomenal. I have found myself standing around to just listen to the beautiful music. 

The gameplay is nearly perfect. I have but three ever so tiny annoyances. Pulling out my climbing axes rarely works right and I end up swinging my arms around like a madman trying to get them to come out. Being able to store them on my body would have been so much easier. Second, when using the weapons bench I end up grabbing that darn paint gun by accident when going for my regular gun. The third is the incredibly limited range that the bad guys have to sense you. I might be standing just ten to twenty feet from a bad guy in broad daylight and they don't know I'm there... it's a little odd. Once they register you, you best be ready to fight. If they catch you off guard you are going down quick. 
So very minimal issues. 

Zero glitches, ZERO! That is mine blowing for a quest game!

Overall this is a story driven, single player, RPG, action, adventure, with Metroid-esque gameplay.  

My favorite sword move, side step baddies swinging hard and low to take them off their feet and then turn and slice their head off before their can get up.  

Also the arrow sniper gun I've set up is amazing! Sometimes I don't use the sword and just charge a bad guy to plug an arrow through his face. Unless there are a lot of them, then I'll thin the herd from a distance. 

This is great, really great! Thanks so much to the developers for giving us a real high end gaming experience.

First psvr2 game I thought to myself while playing , this is incredible but I wish I could play this on quest 3 for freedom of movement and qgo sharpness , then hearing this was coming to q3 I knew I was in for a good time. Game feels so much better to play all around with such light gear and being able to walk around my play space. As a port it's probably one of the best vr ports period and as a game it's quest 3s best title right next to Batman Arkham shadow! Tons of mechanics from melee combat , firearms , swimming , climbing , jumping , awesome sense of progression with fully customizable weapons and is one of the closer quest games to being open world at such high quality! Support the developers and Enjoy 2025 vr goty!

reviews.review_title: A masterful and compelling adventure in
reviews.review_description:
We’re looking at an excellent port of the PCVR version that runs beautifully on the Meta Quest. The game looks great (even better with Quest Games Optimizer), the mechanics are smooth, and the combat is intense and engaging with badas moments. It’s dynamic, makes great use of verticality, and the puzzles are just as satisfying. The gear customization keeps you wanting to do more and unlock everything.
Overall, it’s a very enjoyable experience that will keep you hooked for a good ten hours or more!

I was having a great time with this one.But i've been running around in circles for the last three hours.I'm about done. I have no idea what to do or where to go. I have to update my review. I figured out what I did wrong . back on track giving it 5 stars now.

developer_response.body:
Hey NeonKookaburra, Sorry to hear this, happy to help you figure out where to go next though! Please join our Discord https://discord.gg/AQfR3y7Pry or email us at contact@vitruviusvr.com and we can help. Also, one tip is to turn on Hints on the Map, the blue arrow at the top will always point towards your next main objective. Hope to hear from you so we can get this sorted out! Thanks, Blake -VitruviusVR

Update: Okay glad you figured it out!

reviews.review_description: Honestly I love this game, I wanted this for a while and it didn’t disappoint. The graphics weren’t incredible, but still very nice. The mechanics are insanely good I love everything about it. Overall this is 100% a great purchase on sale or not! This game should’ve been game of the year imo can’t wait to see what the devs have in store for this game in the future.

This game is fantastic. I've played through twice. The combat is great, even though I don't really do combat. It's fun exploring the vividly beautiful areas of the game and the story is engaging. I fell in love with the Nara, especially when they greet you with reverence in the Nara sanctuary for helping their people . This is why this game has a near perfect rating. Part 2 has to be a thing I hope.

Great looking and Great sword mechanics. 
Only problem is the left pick does not flick out very well.

developer_response.body:
Hey The_Richelieu, Sorry to hear this, happy to help you figure this out though! There are  currently no known bugs with the Red Button in-game but please join our Discord https://discord.gg/HhEuMs3Zbv or email us at contact@vitruviusvr.com and we can help. Hope to hear from you so we can get this sorted out! Thanks, Blake -VitruviusVR

Update: Glad you got it working! And for picks you can go to Settings > VR Body and change the "pick-flick" sensitivity, that might help a bit.

reviews.review_description: Arken Age is a true work of art in the realm of virtual reality because of how organic its gameplay experience is. The combat is fully physics-based, so swinging your swords, blocking attacks, and shooting weapons feels so weighty and impactful. You can pick up opponents, scale surfaces, cross chasms, and navigate the environment with your virtual body in ways that make all of this feel organic and unscripted. Upgradable weapons allow you to play around with different styles as you explore the stunning environment of the alien planet. Also you can customize the weapons however you want. Overall, Arken Age is an amazing game in a true masterpiece on the VR platform.

reviews.review_title: Stunning and a joy to play
reviews.review_description: I've just completed this utterly brilliant game. The graphics are so smooth and its the first VR game to actually make my stomach do somersaults when falling from a great height. The weapons and the upgrades are fantastic. The AI is really good too making for some heated battles. The best part is climbing, it is so satisfying and flawless. It's a decent size with an epic end battle to boot. A must have in anyone's VR library. The 1 irritating thing i found was when my melee weapon kept getting stuck against my shield, so be aware.

reviews.review_title: AAA Title for a good reason!
reviews.review_description:
I’m several hours (and more deaths than two hands) into Arken Age and it’s clear this is an AAA title. So much thought went into to every part of this game, from movement to configurabity to user choice of weapons. 

More VR devs should look at what was done right here!
I love this game!! Though Im new to vr gaming but this game is awesome! Very addictive 10/10

reviews.review_title: Cool combat but not worth it
reviews.review_description: The most useful reviews I’ve seen for games are the ones that say the game is fun for free but a waste of money to buy, and this is one of those games. I like the combat, it’s honestly one of my favorite combat engines on vr, and it also has pretty good graphics. My only problem is the story, or the lack of one. They throw together a lackluster reason to complete the game, but throughout the whole game I feel like I’m just running around finding random relics to accomplish nothing. My recommendation is to save your money from this game for a game like Arkham shadow.

It was really fun killing the enemies over and over again, but when I got to the end, I was like, "That's it?"


Would play again, but when an expansion comes out, I haven't tried out the other difficulties, tho

This is a really fantastic port. If you were eyeing the game on PC or PSVR2 but unable to play there, don't hesitate to get the Quest version. It not only looks great, but is far more polished and performant than nearly all VR games.  Tons of great VR interactions, especially regarding combat and customizable weapons, and for once a semi-open world game!  In a sea of linear only titles, it's very refreshing to find there's actual locations to explore and come back to with future upgrades and unlockables.  For ARPG fans looking for something in a similar vein to Asgard's Wrath, this is an easy recommendation.

The enemies have amazing combat. The combat itself feels great,  climbing, the maps, the ART STYLE?! Get in. Arken Age is a truly fantastic game and honestly deserves more love. There should be 5.0 stars on here, not 4.9!!

reviews.review_title: The most immersive game of the year
reviews.review_description: The devs really know how to use the strengths of VR to pull you into their world and make you feel you are a part of it. They also did an incredible job porting a PCVR game to Quest 3 while keeping everything silky smooth.

reviews.review_title: The Age of VR is coming!
reviews.review_description:
And this game is leading the charge! The graphics and physics on Quest 3 are great, and all the weapons available are different and offers a unique play style for each. 
Also, considering the fact you're a lizard-person, you can jump from high ledges without fall damage and breathe underwater; everybody's dream, right?
It also has an extensive weapon customization system, engaging puzzles, and fluid hand-to-hand combat; everything a VR franchise needs for a strong foundation...hopefully the Dev team keep making more games like this 👍

reviews.review_title: Really great. So close to 5 stars
reviews.review_description: The game is beautiful in setting and style and the gameplay is great. I hate to criticize anything but I hope its constructive. It seems like its going to be short at this rate and with such a wonderful backdrop it's a shame there's not much character story interaction to drive the narrative. The only problem i had was with the quick menu feature. It's hard to manage in combat with using the joystick to move. I would prefer most be on body grabs with customizable setups. I keep grabbing ammo from my shoulder when i want my blade. I keep grabbing blade instead of ammo and instead of pulling tablet from my back I keep pushing the wrong quick menu button. I'd love to custom slots. Sword, 2h gun on shoulders. Ammo on left belt. Health and grenades on chest. Tablet from lower back. Too used to this setup in other games.

I like this game very much. I am not a hardcore player, who tries to get at fast as he can at the end. 

I am more a player that investigate everything, try to do all my missions. Slow and steady. Lucky for me that you can choose the difficulty of the game.  ( I choose ‘novice’). 

Exploring everything at ease. Try to collect all relics. And kill the enemies. I enjoyed every second when I was playing. The graphics are so good.

In the beginning I had some problems with the controls. But after a few tries,it went better and better.

Absolutely blown away by pretty much everything, the enemy AI is a joy to see, when they run to take cover, pair up and flank your position, really well done. Graphically it looks gorgeous for standalone VR, many people tend to forget that this is running (incredibly smoothly) on what is in essence a mobile phone strapped to your face. Every single mechanic works flawlessly, in fact sometimes too well, as I accidentally throw valuable items from my inventory and then have to scurry after them lol. The games level area's are huge with crazy amounts of cleverly hidden nooks and crannies. Easily my game of the year so far.
l will be like to play part 2 👍🏻🤞🏻

I have played quite a few VR games, Arken Age is undoubtedly one of the best games I have played so far. It quickly became my favorite...

Everything they set out to do was done with masterful execution. This is the best VR combat I have experienced, from strikes to thrusts, blocks and parrys...all of it looks and feels amazing

The story was engaging and definitely had me right from the beginning...and didn’t let up at all.

The traversal mechanics from climbing to swimming all look and feel great, it is alot of fun to traverse through this world. The environments are breathtaking and very immersive.

Theres a ton of weapon variety and gadgets to use throughout your journey, combat becomes pure cinema.

I can't stress this enough, this game is amazing. I really hope theres more in store for Arken Age in the future.

Hopefully a sequel or DLC, I will gladly take anything at this point

Arken Age is a sure-fire candidate for VR Game of the Year, regardless of platform. The game has everything you need: beautiful graphics, exciting combat, an interesting story and enjoyable exploration. Quest 3 port is fantastic, it looks great and runs very smoothly. Great job, VitruviusVR, I can't wait for your next game :)

reviews.review_title: No wonder why this game ￼won game awards
reviews.review_description: This game is literally the definition of the best. It has good combat. Good graphics and literally a good story. Also not only that but you could customize your weapon to any color and anything you want. This is the best game I’ve ever played if you’re looking for a action game that lets you customize your weapon and has fun combat with good graphics. This game is for you. ￼￼

I was looking for a new game to play, one that was actually entertaining. I stumbled upon this one. I saw it was potentially game of the year, but wondered if it was really my style. I am increasingly glad I tried it. 

Arken Age is such an incredible game. The graphics are good, the controls and gameplay are awesome, and the amount of things you can do is just so mind blowing to me. The fact that I can climb a tree, hang from one hand while firing a gun from the other is just so cool. 

I can confidently say that this phenomenal game is my favorite VR game yet. If you are thinking of getting it, do. It is amazing.
The perfect game ever played

reviews.review_title: I need to add another star!
reviews.review_description: I have been playing for a bout 5 hours and have hardly dented the game. Don't need to play more to give it a 5 star rating. On every count it's top notch. A breath of fresh air in VR. It's like the devs took note of all that works in VR over the years and put it all into one game and added their own stamp on it. Controls and gameplay are excellent. Not a hint of any bugs and this is the first version. It's incredible that such a tiny team can do it right when other, bigger companies release a bug ridden messes that take months to fix
This was my first game on vr and was amazing don’t hesitate to purchase

developer_response.body: That's so awesome to hear! Welcome to the VR community, really glad you enjoyed the game! Thanks, Blake -VitruviusVR

reviews.review_description: I just finished a 100% run of this game on my first play through and I just have to say that this game is definitely a must have, it’s got action and great traversal mechanics as well as I large weapon selection, the story is also very compelling and kept me searching for more every time, 10/10 would play again
Great story, the fighting mechanics, controls, and inventory management make sense. Could be a game I actually go back in for to get all the achievements

reviews.review_title: A New Benchmark for Standalone Headsets
reviews.review_description:
Let’s get this out of the way: yes, Arken Age is great, and yes, it’s absolutely worth your hard-earned money.

This game, in the words of Todd Howard, “just works.”
But unlike Bethesda games… Arken Age actually does just work.

By the time I finished the tutorial, I was already sold. If you’re like me and love pushing the limits of what a VR game will let you do, I’ve got good news—this game will keep surprising you. The mechanics feel natural, responsive, and reliable.

Every time I run into a new group of enemies, I’m asking myself:
“How do I want to take these guys on this time?”
Shield and sword?
Sword and shotgun?
Sword and pistol?
Pistol and shield?
You get the idea. The combat sandbox is deeper than it looks, and experimenting is half the fun.

There’s a lot more I could gush about, but I don’t want to spoil anything. What I will say is this: every developer building for standalone hardware should be taking notes. Arken Age sets a new standard for VR moving forward.

Pick this one up. You won’t regret it
Just loved this game, if you like ,asgart , batman , red matter, then just get this, it is a treat. Thank you developers for a most enjoyable experience.  Look forward to more.

I have no issues with the game other than the amount of content. Don’t get me wrong, there’s still a lot to do and though I haven’t done new-game+ yet, it was still a great experience. However I’m too good at the game, and wish I’d gone through on the hardest difficulty to start with, as I’d be able to go through the game at a normal pace.
 
(Spoilers)

It also feels like there is still a lot more to do, and expand on. The plot twist at the end made me think that I had only done half the campaign, but then it ended there. If the devs see this and plan on expanding the game further, please make it an update to the base game instead of a whole second game. While there’s a lot to do, for me it didn’t feel like as much as it actually is, as I’m one of those people who clear out the entire area before progressing, and because of this it didn’t feel like the whole game. Though, again, I don’t know if new-game+ will have extra content.
(Also, please excuse any bad grammar. I’m typing this on mobile and the keyboard covers the box you write in.)

reviews.review_title: A Must-Play Action Adventure for VR Fans
reviews.review_description:
Arken Age blends science fiction and fantasy into a visually rich, immersive VR experience that thrives on physicality and smart systems. You play as one of the last survivors of a nearly extinct race, exploring a ruined world shaped by ancient technology and catastrophe. While the story won’t break new ground, it’s well-paced and serves as a solid backbone for the game’s real strengths: exploration, puzzle-solving, and fast, fluid combat.

From the outset, Arken Age impresses with its physics-driven gameplay. Its melee system is satisfying without being overly complex, offering punchy combat that rewards timing and movement. Weapon crafting is a standout feature—you start with three weapon slots (melee, sidearm, and two-handed ranged), but these can be transformed through a modular upgrade system that keeps the action feeling fresh. Whether you’re smashing enemies with a brutal energy mace or switching to a precision railgun mid-fight, it’s all smooth and deeply engaging.

What elevates the experience even more is how tactile the world feels. Climbing, mining, swimming, and crafting are all intuitive and make the most of VR’s physical nature. 

Visually, the Quest 3 port is impressive. While textures are dialed back from the PCVR version, the vibrant art style and cohesive world-building remain intact. Arken Age looks and feels like a premium VR title on standalone hardware. Sound effects are stellar—weapon impacts, underwater audio distortion, and spatial cues all shine—though the soundtrack is more subdued than it should be.

While the final third of the game starts to repeat itself a bit and the enemy variety is limited, Arken Age still stands as one of the most polished and complete action-adventure titles on Quest 3. It’s a true VR-native experience, and one well worth your time! 

For our full review, search YouTube for 6DOF Reviews Arken Age!

I just gave this the worst review ever BECAUSE I'M A MORON AND DIDN'T PAY ATTENTION!!!! 

I kept thinking I didn't have a key that I've had the whole time because I didn't realize that you had to actually bring it up out of your tablet. 

Don't make the same mistake. 

Oh my God I'm so stupid lol 

honestly kept saying it was the best game ever like the best everything graphics physics just everything was amazing except, I couldn't get past apart and I figured they just didn't have any more content so they made it impossible.... No No I'm just impossible lol 

sorry devs

Fun, good graphics. ￼Love the customizations. ￼Flow is great. It’s one I would recommend. Just my first impressions though, but I believe when I update this review my mind won’t be changed.￼ I don’t really have anything I can complain about. This is what VR is supposed to be. ￼ hopefully it only gets better.￼ great job everyone on making this one worth it. And the price. Yeah. Definitely worth it.👍 ￼


I’m a couple days in now . This game is outstanding. Very few glitches. ￼ I like that it feels kind of like tomb, raider and out world made a VR baby. It has some stealth aspects. Puzzles you have to think through a little. Fun side quest. I got so much more than what I expected when I bought top 10 list game.

Since beeing in VR (10 years) I thought Metroid Prime would be the best VR experience possible, and this is the closest we got so far.

Now that i played through, i can only hope for a new part with even more budget and slower skill build up, even more big bosses.... but u know that already :)

Unbelievably well done! Bet some retro studio employees had tears in their eyes after playing this 😜

Big Up!!

Fantastic Game, great visuals for the Q3. Long Campaign, I'm about 50% in and been playing for what seems like weeks. Lots of action, puzzles to solve. You need to explore every location. Easily in my top 3 Quest games of all time.

I’m maybe 2-3 hours in but had to write a review, I can tell already that this will be one of my favs.  The visuals, the mechanics, everything is absolutely top notch (and just with the Q3).  Can’t wait to keep going!!!!!
This is it. The best vr campaign I’ve played since half life Alyx. Well worth the price. I’m totally hooked and can’t wait to start new game plus.

This game has a intriguing story and Great gameplay.  I was able to customize the controllers to my liking making the game way more enjoyable. I'm very excited to finish this game and see what comes next

developer_response.body:
Hey Douggernaut, thanks for the feedback! You can actually put the gun on your left-side and sword on right while keeping movement on the right controller. Go into Settings> Controls> and change Controller Layout to Inverted, that will put movement onto your right controller. Then go to Settings>VR Body> and change the Melee Slot and the Light Gun Slot. We have a left-handed player on our Dev Team so we tried to make the game great for southpaws too. Thanks, Blake -VitruviusVR

Update: Glad to hear you got all the settings working to your liking. Hope you enjoy the rest of the game! Thanks, Blake -VitruviusVR

reviews.review_title: Thank you for all of it!!!
reviews.review_description: First of all, thank you for making standalone version of Arken Age, I was little jealous of Steam & Playstation folks. This is one of my 2025 masterpiece & favorites. Worlds are immersive, combats are intense, and graphics are so stunning!!! I was surprised from beginning to ending. Every moments are memorable, soundtrack was atmospheric.

I don’t even know where to start. I’ve been a huge VR fan for a long time and have played tons of games, but this one is truly something special. I had high hopes going in, and it’s already surpassed my expectations. The soundtrack, the atmosphere, the combat—everything just clicks. This game is the full package.

It’s so good I actually had to take my headset off just to write this review. Now I’m about to dive back in and get fully immersed for hours. I don’t even want to rush through it because I’m enjoying every minute of exploring this world.

The combat is incredible using a mix of melee and ranged that both feel very good to use and the ai is great. But combat isn’t the only good thing about this game, the story is highly interesting and the game is surprisingly lengthy taking about 15-20 hours to beat. It also has tons of repeatability from finding all the collectibles or playing ng+. Overall Arken age is one of the best games on quest to date.

The moment I started this game, you can tell it oozes quality… the graphics are so clean and sharp, sounds so perfect for the game.
You start off with doing a tutorial, teaching you how to do all the various aspects of this game… how to shoot, climb, heal, use shields and weapons ect..
Then it’s into the main game… it’s just so impressive how they got so much into this game.
Great physics and rag doll effects, add to the immersion during combat… and the enemies have good AI too, dodging, taking cover ect..

Everything fits so well into the design of the game too, how you store equipment, read your vitals.. your hands as you look at them, actually feel connected to you and move so fluidly with your own movements.

When you start a game and feel the quality instantly like you do on this, you know you have something special in front of you to play and enjoy.

Well done devs for this wonderful port.

Highly recommended!!

reviews.review_title: One of the BEST VR games hands down!!
reviews.review_description: Games like this give me hope for VR. It’s honestly one of the best games on the market right now. It has Halo vibes I love it!! Very well made with attention to detail!

Nothing else on the market is going to compare to this awesome game. Lots of challenges and play time, fully customizable weapons, some minor character customization. I was sad to get to the end of the game but I just started it right back over.

Just a little light on content. The game runs beautifully, graphics are simple but work well, mechanics are mostly great (climbing pick retrieval not being sensitive enough is an exception), but the story is very short and can be completed within a few hours.

Is there a sequel coming?

Now that you can plainly see what talent, passion, dedication and actual love of vr gaming looks like, the rest of you half stepping, cash grabbing, lazy excuse makers need to step up your game. This is REAL vr implementation and POLISH and the fact that this mind blowing epic is developed by a small team should make you ashamed for your janky, low effort ways. Arken Age is the game I imagined I'd be playing when I got a Quest 3. Refunding half effort crap from now on because THIS is what Q3 is capable of. No more excuses. Congratulations Arken devs, you created a masterpiece here that should be the bar to compare all other Meta games to.
